scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated workflows stayed within protein-design and representation-learning scope without claiming experimental validation.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package does not present design candidates as clinically validated biological products.

methodological_ground.detail: Generation, inverse folding, and embeddings were described as model workflows rather than wet-lab conclusions.

code_usability.detail: The included code examples are actionable at a documentation level, subject to local environment setup.

functional_suitability.note: The package is rich in examples and covers the main ESM3 and ESM C workflows well.

reliability.note: The examples are helpful, and the package distinguishes local and hosted paths clearly enough for bounded execution planning.

performance_context.note: The skill acknowledges local versus Forge execution tradeoffs clearly.

agent_usability.note: Examples are strong, though the package behaves more like a workflow note set than a strict agent contract.

human_usability.note: Track-level separation and annotated code examples make it easy for protein-ML practitioners to identify the right entry point.

security.note: Token use is documented, and the package keeps hosted and local workflows operationally separated.

maintainability.note: The package depends on fast-moving upstream APIs and model names.

agent_specific.note: Composability is high, but refusal boundaries and exact invocation discipline are lighter than in stricter skill packages.

key_strengths: The package covers multiple protein-model workflows without collapsing them into a single vague prompt.
Local and hosted execution paths are both documented clearly enough for advanced users.
The examples preserve task-level specificity across generation, inverse folding, and embeddings.

recommendations.title: Add explicit environment and secret-failure recovery notes
recommendations.problem: Real-world failures are more likely to come from CUDA, package, or token issues than from prompt ambiguity.
recommendations.root_cause: The package prioritizes capability coverage over operational failure handling.
recommendations.fix: Add a short troubleshooting section for missing GPU support, invalid Forge tokens, and rate-limit fallback behavior.
